narrative: US tech earnings wreck overnight — IBM fell 7.5% and ServiceNow crashed 18%, dragging NASDAQ -0.89% and creating a strong next-day lag signal. OMX30 trades 3.2% below its 200-MA with MACD in negative territory. Brent crude ticked up to $102.88 on tanker interceptions adding industrial headwinds. Key risk: Swedish CPIF at 09:30 — a softer print could trigger a Riksbank cut rally in banks.
morningBrief: Good morning. Expect OMX30 to open -0.3% to -0.5% on the back of a US tech wreck. IBM -7.5% and ServiceNow -18% will pressure Ericsson and Hexagon at open. Brent at $102.88 adds cost headwinds for industrials. Boliden should outperform — gold $3,210 and copper firm. Key support: 3,085 (lower Bollinger band). Watch CPIF at 09:30 for bank sector direction.
drivers: Brent crude +0.9% to $102.88 on tanker intercepts

sectorImpacts.reason: NASDAQ -0.89% creates sentiment contagion. Ericsson exposed to software capex cycles.
Gold $3,210 +0.6% and copper firm at $4.82/lb — direct revenue drivers for Boliden.
Brent +0.9% adds input cost pressure. Partially offset by EUR/SEK weakness.
Binary event: CPIF at 09:30. Hot print (>2.8%) = Riksbank pause = NIM positive. Soft print = cut expectation = banks down.
US 10Y at 4.42% keeps financing costs elevated. Structural underperform until Riksbank delivers cuts.
Defensive rotation on risk-off. USD strength supportive for USD-denominated revenues.

watchList.reason: Gold $3,210 +0.6%, copper firm — direct revenue upside. Best long setup today.
US tech wreck sentiment. May gap down at open.
Binary on CPIF at 09:30. Hot print = buy, soft print = sell.

econCalendarAlert: Swedish CPIF (09:30 CET) — consensus 2.8% — binary event for bank stocks. US Initial Jobless Claims (14:30 CET).
topRisk: Iran tanker escalation could spike Brent to $110+, crushing consumer and industrial stocks while boosting Boliden.

newsHeadlines.title: IBM shares tumble 7% after cautious full-year guidance
newsHeadlines.description: IBM beat estimates but maintained conservative guidance, disappointing investors.

newsHeadlines.title: ServiceNow crashes 18% as Middle East conflict hits subscription growth
newsHeadlines.description: Enterprise clients in the region paused spending decisions.

newsHeadlines.title: Germany halves growth forecast to 0.5% for 2026, citing Iran conflict
newsHeadlines.description: Berlin now expects Europe's largest economy to expand just half a percent this year.
